home.hero_desc: A modern operating system built on JowoKernel — a 64-bit monolithic kernel developed entirely from scratch. Built for performance, stability, and POSIX compatibility.
home.philosophy_desc: KlatOS development is based on key principles that ensure quality at every layer of the system.
home.arch_desc: All subsystems use modern object-oriented and event-driven architecture. Each component has clear responsibilities and can be developed independently.
home.perf_desc: All critical paths are designed to minimize overhead: zero-copy VMO, event-driven WaitQueue, non-blocking I/O, shared memory IPC, and page flipping.
home.posix_desc: Implements POSIX interfaces so that various applications and libraries can be ported with minimal changes.
home.gfx_desc: Modern DRM-based graphics stack with dumb buffer, page flipping, VBlank synchronization, zero-copy framebuffer, and memory mapped graphics.
home.sec_desc: Per-process virtual address space, process isolation, guard pages, reference counting, automatic resource cleanup, and memory leak detection.
home.clean_desc: The kernel is not built from a collection of patches. Modular design with clear subsystem boundaries, passing unit tests, regression tests, and automated stress tests.
home.kernel_desc: A 64-bit monolithic kernel providing all operating system services to userspace through a stable syscall interface.
home.proc_desc: Multi-process & multi-thread scheduler, context switching, process groups, sessions, job control, signal delivery, orphan & zombie process handling.
home.mem_desc: Physical Memory Manager, VMM, kernel heap, slab allocator, VMO, memory mapping, shared memory, guard pages, copy-on-write ready.
home.fs_desc: Virtual File System, TmpFS, ProcFS, DeviceFS, file descriptor table, symbolic links, hard links, permission management, path resolution.
home.ipc_desc: Pipes, Unix Domain Socket, shared memory, zero-copy buffer sharing, file descriptor passing (SCM_RIGHTS).
home.event_desc: WaitQueue, poll, select, epoll, event-driven wakeup, non-blocking file descriptors — eliminating periodic polling that wastes CPU.
home.desktop_desc: Display server, window manager, input server, desktop shell, launcher, notification service, session manager — all via POSIX IPC.
home.release_desc: Download the latest version of KlatOS

about.hero_desc: A modern operating system developed entirely from scratch
about.vision_p1: KlatOS aims to be a modern desktop platform combining high performance, clean kernel design, POSIX compatibility, and modern graphics architecture in a self-developed operating system.
about.vision_p2: With JowoKernel as its foundation, KlatOS continues to evolve towards a complete ecosystem capable of supporting modern desktop applications, development environments, and open source software through stable and well-documented standard interfaces.
about.dev_desc: All KlatOS code is developed incrementally through validated milestones. Every new feature must:
about.diff1_desc: All core system components are built independently without using kernel code from other operating systems.
about.diff2_desc: Not just a kernel — bootloader, userspace runtime, system libraries, compositor, desktop shell, and application platform.
about.diff3_desc: The transition to an event-driven model eliminates periodic polling that wastes CPU.

download.hero_desc: A modern operating system built on JowoKernel
download.kernel_desc: 64-bit monolithic kernel with process management, memory management, VFS, IPC, and event infrastructure.
download.gfx_desc: DRM device, dumb buffer, page flipping, VBlank sync, libdrm compatibility.
download.desktop_desc: Display server, window manager, input server, desktop shell, launcher.
download.guide2_desc: Set up the KlatOS development environment.
download.guide3_desc: Run KlatOS in QEMU or VirtualBox.

docs.explore_desc: Visit our GitHub repository to see the source code.

roadmap.title: Roadmap
roadmap.desc: KlatOS development journey is divided into several key milestones
roadmap.m1_desc: Kernel initialization, boot loader, and boot infrastructure.
roadmap.m2_desc: Core kernel subsystem implementation: scheduler, memory management, IPC.
roadmap.m3_desc: ELF loader, dynamic linker, C runtime, POSIX ABI, system call interface.
roadmap.m4_desc: Multi-process scheduler, context switching, signal delivery, process groups.
roadmap.m5_desc: POSIX syscall implementation: fork, exec, pipe, socket, mmap, poll, epoll.
roadmap.m6_desc: VFS, TmpFS, ProcFS, DeviceFS, file descriptor table, permission management.
roadmap.m7_title: M7 — GUI Runtime, IPC & Event System
roadmap.m7_desc: Unix Domain Socket, event-driven WaitQueue, zero-copy buffer sharing, SCM_RIGHTS.
roadmap.m8_desc: DRM device, dumb buffer, page flipping, VBlank sync, libdrm compatibility layer.
roadmap.m9_desc: Library integration: libdrm, SDL2, Mesa3D, Wayland, GTK4, WebKitGTK.
roadmap.m9_libs: libdrm, SDL2, Mesa3D, Wayland, GTK4, WebKitGTK

releases.title: Releases
releases.v1_item2: Process Management: multi-process scheduler, signal delivery, job control
releases.v1_item3: Memory Management: PMM, VMM, VMO, slab allocator, guard pages
releases.v1_item4: Filesystem: VFS, TmpFS, ProcFS, DeviceFS, permission management
releases.v1_item5: IPC: Unix Domain Socket, shared memory, SCM_RIGHTS, epoll
releases.v1_item7: Graphics Stack: DRM, dumb buffer, page flipping, VBlank sync
releases.v1_item9: Desktop Platform: display server, window manager, shell, launcher

blog.title: Blog
blog.desc: Latest news and updates from the KlatOS team
blog.post1_desc: We are proud to announce the first stable release of KlatOS. This is a major milestone in our journey to build a modern operating system from scratch.
blog.post2_desc: JowoKernel transitions to an event-driven model. Every filesystem object has an internal WaitQueue to wake threads only when status actually changes.
blog.post3_desc: KlatOS desktop is built as a collection of independent processes: display server, window manager, input server, shell — all via POSIX IPC.
blog.post4_desc: DRM-based graphics stack with dumb buffer, page flipping, VBlank synchronization, and zero-copy framebuffer.
blog.post5_desc: M9 targets integrating libdrm, SDL2, Mesa3D, Wayland, GTK4, and WebKitGTK to expand application compatibility.
blog.post6_desc: JowoKernel is designed with per-process virtual address space, process isolation, guard pages, and automatic resource cleanup.

developers.title: Developers
developers.desc: The team behind KlatOS and JowoKernel development
developers.team1_desc: Developing JowoKernel: process management, memory management, VFS, IPC, event infrastructure, syscall interface.
developers.team2_desc: DRM-based graphics stack: dumb buffer, page flipping, VBlank sync, libdrm compatibility, framebuffer management.
developers.team3_desc: Desktop platform: display server, window manager, input server, desktop shell, launcher, notification service.
developers.team4_desc: Userspace runtime: ELF loader, dynamic linker, C runtime, POSIX ABI, process startup, memory manager.
developers.team5_desc: Process isolation, guard pages, reference counting, automatic resource cleanup, memory leak detection.
developers.team6_desc: Forum moderation, documentation, supporting new contributors, and community management.
developers.unit_desc: Every new feature must pass unit tests before being merged.
developers.regress_desc: Ensuring new features don't break existing functionality.
developers.stress_desc: Automated stress tests to ensure stability under high load.
developers.abi_desc: Not breaking stable ABIs. Backward compatibility is maintained.
